adoption_verdict.rationale: Brilliant patterns to learn from, but no license blocks direct code adoption. Extract concepts and methodology, don't import as a dependency.

candidates.name: program.md Agent Instruction Pattern
candidates.description: Structured AI agent skill file with setup protocol, experiment loop (keep/discard), autonomy instructions (NEVER STOP), crash recovery, output parsing, TSV logging. The most complete open-source example of an autonomous research agent instruction set.
candidates.relevance_to_sonash: Directly applicable to JASON-OS agent skills. Compare against sonash SKILL.md format. The experiment loop pattern (modify, run, evaluate, keep/discard) is generalizable beyond ML training.

candidates.name: Fixed-Budget Experimentation Pattern
candidates.description: Fixed 5-minute time budget makes experiments comparable regardless of what changes. Decouples 'how long' from 'how good'. Single metric (val_bpb) as ground truth. Git branch per experiment run with keep/discard semantics.
candidates.relevance_to_sonash: Applicable to any automated optimization loop. Could be adapted for performance benchmarking, prompt optimization, or configuration tuning.

candidates.name: 3-File Architecture (Immutable + Mutable + Instructions)
candidates.description: prepare.py (read-only infrastructure) + train.py (agent-editable target) + program.md (instructions). Clear contract: agent knows exactly what it can and cannot modify. Reduces search space for autonomous agents.
candidates.relevance_to_sonash: Pattern for any agent-driven code modification workflow. Defines clear 'editable zones' to constrain agent autonomy.

candidates.name: Results TSV Logging Convention
candidates.description: Simple TSV format: commit hash, metric, memory, status (keep/discard/crash), description. Untracked by git. Human-readable experiment log.
candidates.relevance_to_sonash: Pattern exists in sonash already (JSONL). TSV is simpler but less structured. Low extraction value.

candidates.name: Autonomous Crash Recovery Protocol
candidates.description: Agent reads stack trace on crash, attempts fix, reverts if unfixable. Timeout detection (>10 min = kill). Never-stop semantics. Explicit instructions for crash vs discard vs fix decisions.
candidates.relevance_to_sonash: Relevant for GSD executor, background agent error recovery, and autonomous debugging loops.
